The SC then extracted a +35% increase from ITOCHU's lowball ¥9,611 opening proposal (which offered only a 3.34% premium) to ¥13,000. Exhibit for how a leak/activist pressure destroys a controlling shareholder's ability to open low.

Family: parent-sub buy-in wave (JP-087, JP-042, JP-051, JP-108). Follow-up: identify the activist fund.

deal_structure_raw: PARTIAL tender offer at ¥435/share with BOTH a maximum (15,527,400 sh / 45.00%) and a minimum limit; Fujitsu (58.82% parent) agreed to tender ALL its shares but Silitech will only purchase up to the 45% cap — so Fujitsu exits control while RETAINING a residual stake and a brand-license relationship; FDK deconsolidated from Fujitsu
initial_offer_price_bumps_raw: Final proposal ¥425/share @ 40% max (2024/12/20) → Fujitsu rejected on total-proceeds grounds (2024/12/27) → amended ¥425 @ 45% max (2025/01/10) → final ¥435 @ 45% (1 price bump + 1 size bump — Fujitsu negotiated on TOTAL consideration, not just per-share)
board_recommendation_raw: For (FDK board expressed opinion in favour; noted brand license from Fujitsu continues and business will proceed without particular changes)
competing_bid_raw: No — but the Tender Offer Agreement contained an explicit COUNTER-TOB carve-out permitting Fujitsu to accept a superior competing bid (defined as ≥3% higher on total-consideration basis, made ≥5 business days before the TOB deadline)
toehold_raw: Fujitsu 58.82% (20,295,422 sh) — Tender Offer Agreement commits Fujitsu to tender ALL shares, but the 45% purchase cap means Fujitsu retains a residual stake post-deal and remains a major shareholder
rules_regulations_triggering_review_raw: FIEA TOB (partial offer with max & min limits); inbound foreign acquisition of a Japanese listed subsidiary (FEFTA screening likely — classification TBV)
key_debate_points_raw: Fujitsu's portfolio-transformation programme (same strategic driver as Shinko Electric, JP-010, and Fujitsu General, JP-056); the deal was sourced via PSA Group intermediation with SMBC Nikko as Fujitsu's FA; the negotiation is instructive — Fujitsu pushed back not on PRICE PER SHARE but on TOTAL PROCEEDS, extracting a larger purchase cap (40%→45%) rather than a higher price, then a modest ¥425→¥435 bump. The contractual counter-TOB carve-out is a notable market-check mechanism.
final_resolution_mechanism_raw: Partial TOB; FDK deconsolidated from Fujitsu; FDK remains listed (no squeeze-out/delisting — this is a control transfer, not a take-private)
verification_status_raw: Verified via primary sources (new-deal pass 2026/07/12); TOB result, completion date, premium, and FEFTA classification all TBV — needs a follow-up pass
notes_raw: NEW DEAL 2026/07/12 (owner-approved, 'Fujitsu-adjacent'). BELOW the >US$100mn scope floor (≈¥6.8bn / ~$45mn purchase) — RETAINED under the precedent-value override (Japan_Landmark_Criteria.md §3) for three reasons: (1) novel PARTIAL-TOB-with-cap structure where the parent tenders 100% but can only sell 45%, deliberately leaving a residual stake + brand license; (2) explicit CONTRACTUAL COUNTER-TOB carve-out (≥3% superior, ≥5 bd before deadline) as a built-in market check — a mechanism worth tracking; (3) completes the FUJITSU PORTFOLIO-TRANSFORMATION FAMILY with Shinko Electric (JP-010, ¥685bn to JIC) and Fujitsu General (JP-056, ¥256bn to Paloma Rheem) — three different structures for shedding three listed subsidiaries. Inbound Taiwanese strategic acquirer (cf. Yageo/Shibaura JP-044). FOLLOW-UP: TOB result, completion date, unaffected price/premium, FEFTA classification.
